title: Java Console Input
description: Practice Scanner and System.console input paths for console data.
learningGoals: Read console text with Scanner when consuming System.in.
Use System.console() defensively because it can be null in IDE and redirected runs.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens when System.console() returns null?
choices: console.readLine() safely returns an empty string
The ternary expression uses the Scanner fallback value
Scanner is closed automatically by System.console()
The program cannot compile because Console is abstract
completionChecks.explanation: Many IDE and redirected runs have no Console object. The null check avoids calling readLine on null and falls back to text already read by Scanner.

completionChecks.prompt: Which call reads a full line from the Console object?
completionChecks.explanation: Console exposes readLine for line-oriented console input, optionally with a prompt. Scanner reads from System.in, while System.in itself has no readLine method.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens when console is not null and the user enters Ava at the Console prompt?
choices: name is set to the Scanner fallback value
name is set to Ava after console.readLine("Name: ") returns
name is always an empty string because console input is disabled
The ternary expression evaluates both branches and throws
completionChecks.explanation: The ternary operator evaluates only the selected branch. With a real Console, readLine supplies the name, so the Scanner fallback is not used for name.
